text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on rotation, metals dumped, defensives bid. Fear & Greed at 60.1 with GLD -3.02% and SLV -7.09% shows profit-taking in crowded hedges while small caps and cyclicals caught a bid against still-greedy sentiment. SPY -0.05% with IJR +1.16% and XLI +1.16% says this was a positioning reset out of megacap growth and precious metals into domestic cyclicals and defensives rather than outright de-risking.

INDICES — VIX 18.6, slightly above its 20-day 17.7 and below the 52-week peak 31.1, keeps volatility elevated but controlled. Rank: IJR +1.16% > RSP +0.71% > IJH +0.61% > IWM +0.46% > DIA +0.37% > SPY -0.05% > SOXX -0.31% > QQQ -0.42% > GLD -3.02% > SLV -7.09%. STYLE: SMALL > LARGE by 1.21pp (small +1.16% vs large -0.05%). The standout cross-asset move was USO -4.47% dragging SLV -7.09% and GLD -3.02% in a broad commodities flush.

VOLUME — SPY volume was roughly neutral with the index -0.05%, implying a low-conviction pause rather than forceful selling, while QQQ volume ran below the 1.28x index average as QQQ fell -0.42%, signaling corrective tech weakness without panic. SLV 2.4x, IJR 1.8x, and GLD 1.6x showed intense focus on metals liquidation and small-cap accumulation, while SOXX 0.8x and XLK 0.8x indicated fading enthusiasm in semis and tech despite modest declines.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLI Industrials +1.16%, XLY Discretionary +1.15%, XLU Utilities +1.04%, XLP Staples +0.86%, XLV Healthcare +0.77%, XLB Materials +0.57%, XLRE Real Estate -0.29%, XLF Financials -0.30%, XLK Technology -0.62%, XLC Comms -0.68%, XLE Energy -1.63%. Top industries: ARKG +7.14% and XHB +5.62%; bottom: SILJ -4.34% and WGMI -5.95%. Money is rotating into industrials and discretionary while exiting energy and communication services, with XLE -1.63% and XLC -0.68% underperforming against XLI +1.16% and XLY +1.15%. With broad sector participation on the upside and AI-linked XLK only modestly red at -0.62%, today fits a broadening participation tape rather than narrow AI leadership, so call it broadening participation.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR 0.78 with ATM IV 16.2% and positive skew 10.5% shows moderate call demand with some residual put premium, i.e., no panic but still paying for downside. QQQ PCR 1.25 and skew 15.0% alongside VXN 30.2 above its 20-day 26.9 flag active protection in tech, while IWM PCR 1.08 with RVX 24.6 roughly in line with its 20-day 24.1 reflects renewed small-cap interest but still hedged exposure.

CROSS-ASSET — Dollar strength via UUP +0.28% alongside GLD -3.02% and SLV -7.09% underscores a classic strong-USD, weak-precious-metals day. The simultaneous rally in TLT +1.37% and IEF +0.65% against USO -4.47% points to an inflation-scare reset with bond bids returning while energy unwinds.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 60.1 (Greed) with SLV -7.09% (metals), ARKG +7.14% (healthcare innovation), and XHB +5.62% (homebuilders) marking the biggest thematic movers, while XLE -7.40% 20d and XLC -7.80% 20d define downside momentum extremes versus SOXX +5.51% 20d and IJR +3.46% 20d on the upside. Vol spikes in XTN 31.8x with price +0.39% suggest stealth accumulation in transports, while SCHH 5.5x with -0.63% smells like distribution in REITs. Watch IJJ +0.89% with 5.0x volume for confirmation of a mid-cap value leadership turn, SCHH -0.63% for follow-through stress in rate-sensitive real estate, and XLC -0.68% with 1.4x sector volume as a potential oversold rebound candidate if communication services selling exhausts.
